text: When working with Git, branching is an essential part of an effective version control workflow. A common practice is to create feature branches for new tasks. Instead of editing the main code directly, developers work on a separate branch until the feature is complete. Once ready, the changes are integrated through merging. Typically, this process involves a pull request, where team members review the code before it is merged into the main branch. This approach keeps the main code stable, allows multiple people to work simultaneously, and ensures high quality through collaboration and review.
